title: Null Mnemonics
text: Null Mnemonics The ^NUL mnemonic (and its equivalents ^0 and ^/0 ) is interpreted as a delimiter. It is often used to explicitly disable a particular function. Omitting a line that defines a feature is often not sufficient to disable the feature because a default definition is then used. Putting ^NUL as the last character in any output sequence disables the feature, because anything after the ^NUL is ignored. It is also possible to send a NULL value to a printer. To do so, specify %30(0) in the device translation table: %30(0) ^NUL Copy Once this is entered, you can send, for example, ESC^45^NULL to the printer, using the value ESC^45^%30(0) . For example, if a device does not support any character shapes (such as underlined characters), the corresponding video attributes should not be used. Include the following lines in the device translation table definition: ^nul underline ^^ disable all shapes in this set ^nul italic ^nul ita_und ^nul bold ^nul bol_und ^nul bol_ita ^nul bol_ita_und Copy Was this topic helpful?
